name: SOLITARY BONE CYST

demographics.Frequency: up to 5% of primary bone lesions
demographics.Etiology: ? trauma (synovial entrapment at capsular reflection), ? vascular anomaly (blockage
demographics.Histo: cyst filled with clear yellowish fluid often under pressure, wall lined with fibrous
demographics.Age: 3-19 years (80%); occurs during active phase of bone growth; M÷F = 3÷1

dominant_finding: 2-3 cm oval radiolucency with long axis parallel to long axis of host bone
discriminating_features: 2-3 cm oval radiolucency with long axis parallel to long axis of host bone
scalloping + erosion of internal aspect of underlying cortex
photopenic area on bone scan (if not fractured)
"fallen fragment" sign if fractured (20%) = centrally dislodged fragment falls into a dependent
